changelog: Fixed the real reason Reset to Default still didn't work after v14: the SSH addon's own init-ssh script hard-exits (crashing the whole container) if BOTH ssh.password and ssh.authorized_keys are empty -- exactly the addon's normal locked-down steady state on every real customer device. v14's fix started the addon blind, without ensuring a credential was set first, so it crashed before the reset watcher ever got a chance to run. Both places that start the addon (shell_command.dev_reset, and first_boot.sh's maintenance-check branch) now inject a credential first. Also added two layers of defense-in-depth: Supervisor's own watchdog is now enabled on the SSH addon (auto-restarts it on any future crash), and the reset trigger's staleness window widened from 1 to 5 minutes so a slow addon start has more room before a real click gets silently discarded. Separately: some networks block outbound DNS-over-TLS (port 853), which is what Supervisor's DNS plugin uses by default -- this silently breaks push notifications, update checks, and anything else needing a fresh outbound connection, even though the site's own plain DNS works fine for every other device on it. Every boot now explicitly configures plain (port 53) DNS servers -- the site's own gateway plus Cloudflare/Google as fallback -- and disables Supervisor's separate, hardcoded DoT fallback path.
warnings: HA will restart automatically after this update to load the new shell_command definition.
